article_title: GitHub Actions Custom Runner Images Hit General Availability, Letting Teams Bake Toolchains Into Versioned VM Snapshots

content_preview.title: GitHub Actions Custom Runner Images Hit General Availability, Letting Teams Bake Toolchains Into Versioned VM Snapshots
content_preview.summary: GitHub moved custom runner images out of preview on March 26, exposing a snapshot keyword that turns a workflow run into a versioned VM image — and Copilot's own cloud agent cut another 20 percent off its cold start using it.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GitHub Actions custom runner images reached general availability on March 26, 2026, ending a five-month public preview that began in October 2025. The feature, announced on the [GitHub Changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-03-26-custom-images-for-github-hosted-runners-are-now-generally-available/), lets teams take a GitHub-curated base image, install their own tools, certificates, and dependencies during a workflow run, and capture the resulting virtual machine state as a ve...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong technical News piece. Primary-sourced from GitHub changelog + InfoQ. All technical specifics verified.

source_verification.https://github.blog/changelog/2026-03-26: source-0.html — March 26, 2026 GA. Verified: 'GitHub-curated base image', larger runners restriction.
source_verification.https://www.infoq.com/news/2026/04/githu: source-1.html — Verified verbatim: 'snapshot keyword is the core mechanism', 3-step process, Linux x64/Linux ARM64/Windows x64 platform support, weekly image generation recommendation, 'managed artifacts' framing, Team/Enterprise tier restriction.
source_verification.https://github.blog/changelog/2026-04-27: source-2.html — Verified: '20 percent faster' Copilot startup, 50% prior March improvement, prebuilding language, Agents tab/@copilot trigger.
